title: "Data Preparation"

::: {.column-screen .bg-light .p-5 .mb-5 .border-bottom}
📊 Data Preparation Process

Welcome to the data engine room of the LetitHappen team. On this page, we document the entire technical lifecycle of the dataset used to analyze the evolution of Industrial Engineering departments in Turkey between 2018 and 2025—from sourcing and extraction to cleaning and final preparation.
:::

🎯 Strategy and Sources

The foundation of our project is built upon principles of scientific transparency and reproducibility.

::: {.callout-note icon="false"}
🏫 Primary Source: YÖK Atlas

Our analysis relies on official placement statistics from the "Lisans Atlası" (Undergraduate Atlas) module of YÖK Atlas. This is the most reliable database for metrics such as base scores, success rankings, and academic staff counts in Turkey.
:::

::: {.callout-tip icon="false"}
🛠 Extraction Tool: thestats R Package

To import data into our R environment, we utilized the thestats package developed by M. Çavuş and O. Aydın. This tool allows us to query YÖK Atlas data dynamically, ensuring our analysis remains updated with the latest 2025 statistics.
:::

📖 Data Dictionary

The key variables we focused on during our analysis are defined in the table below:

Variable

Description

Type

univ_name

Name of the University

Categorical

u_type

Type of Institution (State / Foundation)

Categorical

city

The city where the university is located

Categorical

base_score

The minimum score of the last student placed

Numerical

success_rank

The national ranking of the last student placed

Numerical

staff_count

Total academic staff (Prof, Assoc. Prof, etc.)

Numerical

erasmus_traffic

Student traffic participating in Erasmus programs

Numerical

🛠 Data Collection & Preprocessing

Transforming raw data into an "analysis-ready" format was performed under the principle of "clean data, accurate results."

1.  Data Import

The following code block demonstrates how the data was retrieved using the thestats package.

#\| label: setup-data #\| message: false #\| warning: false #\| code-fold: true #\| code-summary: "Show R Code (Data Extraction)"

# Loading required libraries

library(thestats) library(tidyverse)

# Structural example of the data retrieval process (2018-2025):

# data_raw \<- list_score(2018:2025) %\>%

# filter(str_detect(program_name, "Endüstri Mühendisliği"))

print("Data retrieval infrastructure is ready.")

2.  Cleaning Steps

To enhance the quality of our analysis, we followed these preprocessing steps:

Filtering: We narrowed our focus specifically to "Industrial Engineering" programs.

Handling Missing Values (NAs): Newly established departments without a base score or those with unfilled quotas were excluded from trend analyses to prevent statistical bias.

Standardization: Spelling discrepancies in city names and university types were corrected for consistency.

Data Transformation: Success rankings and scores were converted to numeric formats to be used in mathematical modeling.

💾 Final Dataset

You can download our processed and analysis-ready dataset via the link below. This file serves as the foundation for all visualizations on our Analysis page.

::: callout-important
📥 Download Final Data: letithappen_data.RData

Note: This link will become active once you upload the .RData file to your GitHub repository.
:::

::: {.border .p-3 .bg-light .rounded}
📝 Ethics Statement and AI Usage

AI Usage: The visual design, table structures, and technical descriptions of this page were optimized with the assistance of Gemini 2.5 Flash.

Human Intelligence (HI) Statement: All generated technical content and data definitions were reviewed and verified by Yusuf Bora Çavdar and Onur Furkan Gök to ensure accuracy and alignment with EMU430 course standards.
:::
